commands.name: tar
commands.description: Archive files together, optionally with compression

patterns.text: create a compressed tar archive of a directory
patterns.explanation: Creates a gzip-compressed tar archive of the directory

patterns.text: extract a tar.gz archive
patterns.explanation: Decompresses and extracts all files from the gzip tar archive

patterns.text: list the contents of a tar archive without extracting
patterns.explanation: Shows all file paths inside the archive

patterns.text: extract a tar archive to a specific directory
patterns.explanation: Extracts the archive contents into /opt/app

patterns.text: create a bzip2-compressed tar archive
patterns.explanation: Creates a tar archive with bzip2 compression for better ratio

patterns.text: extract a single file from an archive
patterns.explanation: Pulls only file.txt out of the archive

commands.name: gzip
commands.description: Compress files using the Lempel-Ziv coding

patterns.text: compress a file with gzip
patterns.explanation: Compresses the file in place, creating access.log.gz and removing the original

patterns.text: compress a file and keep the original
patterns.explanation: Creates data.csv.gz while keeping data.csv intact

patterns.text: compress with maximum compression level
patterns.explanation: Uses the best compression ratio at the cost of more CPU time

patterns.text: compress all files in a directory
patterns.explanation: Recursively compresses every file in the directory

patterns.text: show compression statistics
patterns.explanation: Displays the compression ratio and original file size

patterns.text: decompress a gzip file
patterns.explanation: Restores the original file and removes the .gz compressed version

patterns.text: decompress a file while keeping the compressed version
patterns.explanation: Extracts data.csv while leaving data.csv.gz in place

patterns.text: decompress to standard output for piping
patterns.explanation: Streams the decompressed SQL directly into the database

patterns.text: test if a gzip file is intact without extracting
patterns.explanation: Verifies the integrity of the compressed file

commands.name: bzip2
commands.description: Compress files using the Burrows-Wheeler block sorting algorithm

patterns.text: compress a file with bzip2
patterns.explanation: Creates largefile.txt.bz2 with better compression than gzip

patterns.text: decompress a bzip2 file
patterns.explanation: Decompresses the file back to its original form

patterns.text: compress a file and keep the original
patterns.explanation: Creates database.sql.bz2 while preserving database.sql

patterns.text: check the integrity of a bzip2 compressed file
patterns.explanation: Tests whether the compressed file is valid without extracting

patterns.text: decompress to standard output for piping
patterns.explanation: Decompresses and counts the lines without creating a file

commands.name: xz
commands.description: Compress files using the LZMA2 algorithm for high compression ratios

patterns.text: compress a file with xz for maximum compression
patterns.explanation: Compresses the file with the highest compression level

patterns.text: decompress an xz file
patterns.explanation: Restores the original uncompressed file

patterns.text: compress a file and keep the original
patterns.explanation: Creates firmware.bin.xz while keeping the original file

patterns.text: compress using multiple CPU threads for speed
patterns.explanation: Uses all available CPU cores to speed up compression

patterns.text: show compression info for an xz file
patterns.explanation: Displays compression ratio, check type, and stream information

commands.name: zip
commands.description: Package and compress files into a zip archive

patterns.text: create a zip archive of files
patterns.explanation: Compresses file1.txt and file2.txt into archive.zip

patterns.text: create a zip archive of an entire directory
patterns.explanation: Recursively zips the entire directory tree into project.zip

patterns.text: create a password-protected zip archive
patterns.explanation: Prompts for a password and encrypts the zip archive

patterns.text: add files to an existing zip archive
patterns.explanation: Appends newfile.txt to the existing archive

patterns.text: create a zip excluding certain file patterns
patterns.explanation: Zips the project directory while excluding git files and macOS metadata

commands.name: unzip
commands.description: Extract files from a zip archive

patterns.text: extract all files from a zip archive
patterns.explanation: Extracts all contents into the current directory

patterns.text: extract a zip archive to a specific directory
patterns.explanation: Extracts all contents into the /opt/app directory

patterns.text: list contents of a zip file without extracting
patterns.explanation: Shows all files and their sizes inside the archive

patterns.text: extract a single file from a zip archive
patterns.explanation: Extracts only config.yaml from the archive

patterns.text: extract and overwrite existing files without prompting
patterns.explanation: Overwrites existing files silently during extraction

commands.name: 7z
commands.description: Compress and decompress files using the 7-Zip format with high compression

patterns.text: create a 7z archive with maximum compression
patterns.explanation: Creates a 7z archive with the highest compression level

patterns.text: extract a 7z archive
patterns.explanation: Extracts all files preserving the directory structure

patterns.text: list contents of a 7z archive
patterns.explanation: Shows all files and their sizes inside the archive

patterns.text: create a password-protected 7z archive
patterns.explanation: Encrypts the archive contents and the file names with a password

patterns.text: extract a 7z archive to a specific directory
patterns.explanation: Extracts the archive contents into /opt/extracted

commands.name: zcat
commands.description: View contents of gzip compressed files without extracting

patterns.text: view the contents of a compressed file without extracting
patterns.explanation: Outputs the decompressed content to the terminal

patterns.text: pipe compressed file contents into another command
patterns.explanation: Shows the first 20 lines of the compressed CSV without extracting

patterns.text: concatenate multiple compressed files
patterns.explanation: Decompresses and merges multiple gzipped files into one output

patterns.text: count lines in a compressed log file
patterns.explanation: Counts lines in the compressed syslog without extracting to disk

commands.name: zless
commands.description: View compressed files page by page like the less command

patterns.text: browse a compressed log file interactively
patterns.explanation: Opens the compressed file in a pager for scrolling through content

patterns.text: read a compressed documentation file
patterns.explanation: Views the compressed changelog without extracting it

patterns.text: page through a large compressed CSV file
patterns.explanation: Allows scrolling through the compressed data file interactively

patterns.text: view a compressed text file with search capability
patterns.explanation: Opens the file in a pager where you can search with / like in less

commands.name: zgrep
commands.description: Search for patterns inside compressed files without extracting

patterns.text: search for a pattern in a compressed log file
patterns.explanation: Finds all lines containing ERROR in the compressed syslog

patterns.text: search case-insensitively in a compressed file
patterns.explanation: Matches warning, WARNING, Warning, etc. in the compressed file

patterns.text: count occurrences of a pattern in compressed files
patterns.explanation: Counts 404 errors in each compressed access log file

patterns.text: search for a regex pattern across multiple compressed files
patterns.explanation: Searches for any of the three patterns across all compressed log files

patterns.text: show line numbers of matches in a compressed file
patterns.explanation: Displays matching lines with their line numbers in the original file

patterns.text: create a RAR archive of a directory
patterns.explanation: Compresses the directory into archive.rar

patterns.text: create a password-protected RAR archive
patterns.explanation: Creates an encrypted archive where both data and filenames are protected

patterns.text: create a multi-volume RAR archive split into parts
patterns.explanation: Splits the archive into 100 MB volumes for easier transport

patterns.text: add recovery records to a RAR archive for error correction
patterns.explanation: Adds 5% recovery data so the archive can repair minor corruption

patterns.text: extract all files from a RAR archive
patterns.explanation: Extracts all files preserving the full directory structure

patterns.text: extract a RAR archive to a specific directory
patterns.explanation: Extracts all contents into the /opt/output directory

patterns.text: list contents of a RAR archive
patterns.explanation: Shows all files and their sizes without extracting

patterns.text: test the integrity of a RAR archive
patterns.explanation: Verifies that all files in the archive can be extracted correctly

patterns.text: extract files without preserving directory paths
patterns.explanation: Extracts all files into a single directory ignoring archive folder structure

commands.name: pigz
commands.description: Parallel implementation of gzip that uses multiple CPU cores

patterns.text: compress a large file using all CPU cores
patterns.explanation: Compresses the file using all available cores for much faster speed

patterns.text: compress a file with a specific number of threads
patterns.explanation: Uses 4 threads for parallel compression

patterns.text: decompress a gzip file using multiple cores
patterns.explanation: Decompresses the file using parallel processing for speed

patterns.text: compress with maximum compression and keep the original
patterns.explanation: Best compression using all cores while preserving the original file

patterns.text: use pigz with tar for fast parallel archive creation
patterns.explanation: Pipes tar output through pigz for parallel gzip compression

commands.name: lz4
commands.description: Extremely fast compression and decompression tool

patterns.text: compress a file extremely fast with lz4
patterns.explanation: Compresses the file at very high speed, creating largefile.bin.lz4

patterns.text: decompress an lz4 file
patterns.explanation: Decompresses the file back to its original form

patterns.text: compress with higher ratio at the cost of some speed
patterns.explanation: Uses the highest compression level available in lz4

patterns.text: compress and keep the original file
patterns.explanation: Creates snapshot.img.lz4 while keeping the original file

patterns.text: benchmark lz4 compression speed on a file
patterns.explanation: Runs a compression and decompression speed benchmark

commands.name: zstd
commands.description: Compress and decompress files using the Zstandard algorithm

patterns.text: compress a file with zstandard
patterns.explanation: Creates datafile.bin.zst with a good balance of speed and compression

patterns.text: decompress a zstandard file
patterns.explanation: Decompresses the file back to its original form

patterns.text: compress with maximum compression level
patterns.explanation: Uses compression level 19 for the highest ratio

patterns.text: compress using multiple threads for speed
patterns.explanation: Uses all available CPU cores for parallel compression

patterns.text: compress and keep the original file
patterns.explanation: Creates the compressed file while preserving the original

patterns.text: use zstd with tar for fast compressed archives
patterns.explanation: Creates a tar archive compressed with zstandard for fast speed and good ratio
